question: USER ONBOARDING JOURNEY: Design the complete first-time user experience for Alpha-Omega. Current problems: (1) Prerequisites are heavy — user needs Python + claude CLI + codex CLI + auth for both. ao doctor checks but doesn't help install. (2) ao init creates AGENTS.md but NOT CLAUDE.md — Alpha has no project context. (3) No first 'aha moment' — user must invent their own question, first debate takes 3-6 min, bad question = bad result. (4) 'ao' with no args shows dry help, no welcome or quickstart. (5) No guidance on when to use debate vs review. Research best practices from similar CLI tools: How does Claude Code itself onboard? How does Codex CLI onboard? How do tools like prettier, eslint, husky do first-run? What makes a CLI tool 'sticky' from first use? Design the ideal journey: pip install → first value in under 5 minutes. Consider: Should ao init generate a skeleton CLAUDE.md? Should there be a canned first debate? Should 'ao' with no args run an interactive quickstart? Should ao doctor offer to install missing tools? Should ao init auto-run doctor first? What about a single 'ao setup' command that does everything? Keep it simple — stdlib only, no interactive TUI. But make the text output guide the user step by step.
